one_line_summary: Exemplary submission: complete, atomic Lua-backed Redis impl, thorough ADR/design, and full adversarial pass rate.
strengths: Lua scripts use Redis server time for atomic, skew-resistant enforcement
ADR and design doc cover algorithms, concurrency, clock-skew, and rejected alternatives in depth
All hidden adversarial tests pass (5/5) with clean concurrency discipline
weaknesses: Property test is skipped locally when hypothesis isn't installed rather than declared as a dep
InMemoryBackend uses wall-clock time() which could regress under NTP adjustments
Per-key lock dict grows unbounded (no eviction) for long-lived processes
